evidence_quotes.quote: video games that they have bought after support for those games end
which is currently only able to be played if you can connect to their server
If you can't, you can't play it, even if you have bought it.
You could play it offline, without being dependent on whether the servers were online or whether you could connect to the servers in the first place.

paragraphs.text: Hello, Would you mind considering adding protections that ensure that customers can still play video games that they have bought after support for those games end? After all, why should I not be able to play a game I have bought just because the company no longer officially supports it? Microsoft does not officially support Windows 95 anymore. Yet, you can still install it in a virtualbox or on old hardware. I am worried that many video games I have bought might not be playable in the future. This includes, for example, Hitman 3: World of Assassinations, which is currently only able to be played if you can connect to their server. If you can't, you can't play it, even if you have bought it. Therefore, if they were to discontinue their servers entirely, which is very likely to happen at some point or another, I will not be able to play a video game I have bought. This was not the case with the first game in the trilogy; you did not have to be connected to the internet to play it. You could play it offline, without being dependent on whether the servers were online or whether you could connect to the servers in the first place. If this was possible for the first game, I see no reason why the game should be unplayable after support for it ends. Thank you.
